name: Reverse Corte
preceding_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Natural Spin Turn, Outside Spin, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Natural Turn, Fallaway Whisk, Back Whisk, Closed Impetus, Back Lock, Outside Change, Open Impetus, Outside Spin
notes:
Note: On step 2 turn may be made on ball of RF with foot flat. Footwork would then be 1. THT; 2 Toes both feet; 3 TH(RF)
Note: Other alignments (between steps 1-2) are the following, with different ending alignment:
backing LOD, 1/2 L between 1-2, ending against LOD
backing LOD, 5/8 L between 1-2, ending BDW against LOD
backing DC, 1/4 to L between 1-2, ending BDC against LOD.
Follower
Note: When Man uses different alignments as above, the Lady will turn:
Just over 1/4 to L between 1-2, just under 1/4 to L between 2-3
3/8 to L between 1-2, 1/4 to L between 2-3
1/8 to L between 1-2, 1/8 to L between 2-3

steps.action: RF back | BLOD | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM
comments: Do not step sideways, straight back, use CBM

steps.action: closes to RF without weight | BDC against LOD (or BDC on new LOD) | H(LF) then toes both feet
comments: Drag LF in with heel. No rondes. Keep feet flat, keep knees flexed. Do not pop up both toes too early. Stay down till lady gets on her RF, then rise.
Lower down through the right foot.
Step 1 of all following figures would be CBMP, Lady OP.

steps.action: LF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 1
comments: This is the same footwork as second half of Reverse Turn.
Use CBM to help with the turn and stay together with Man.
Do not drive using upper body. Connect shoulder with hip joint, release hip joint forward which starts the motion.

steps.action: RF to side | Facing Center | T | 1/4 turn between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Continue to rise on 2 and 3
comments: Do not step too big, it will pull man off his feet.
Stay very left. Connect L shoulder down. If spine travels to the right, it will pull weight over to right and off balance.

steps.action: LF closes to RF | DC against LOD | TH
comments: Gracefully collect and get back (especially when extensions last extra measure.)
